sanskrit:
अविनाशि तु तद्विद्धि येन सर्वमिदं ततम्।
विनाशमव्ययस्यास्य न कश्चित्कर्तुमर्हति॥
transliteration:
avināśi tu tad viddhi yena sarvam idaṃ tatam |
vināśam avyayasyāsya na kaścit kartum arhati ||
literal_meaning: Know that to be indestructible by which all this is pervaded; no one can cause the destruction of that imperishable.
contextual_meaning: Krishna reassures Arjuna that the core of identity—consciousness itself—cannot be harmed, giving psychological permission to act without fear of annihilation.
psychological_state: Cognitive shift from ego-threat to trans-ego safety; existential anxiety is met with a pointer toward the changeless witness within.
trigger_scenario: A soldier about to deploy, a patient facing major surgery, or anyone having the 3 a.m. panic of "what if I cease to be?"
anchor_text: Your essence is unbreakable; act with courage, knowing nothing can destroy the real you.
